title: TreeRecurrence
summary: Defines paths, descendants, subtree weights, defect coefficients, and the first recurrence differential for a left-comb tree.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines paths, descendants, subtree weights, defect coefficients, and the first recurrence differential for a left-comb tree.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows PolyGJMSoperators/TreeRecurrence.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Coefficient space of total tree degree k. In explicit terms, for r, k in the natural numbers, its defining expression is Comb Index r k maps to the real numbers.

Vertex corresponding to the j-th leaf. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, and j of type fin r, its defining expression is ⟨ r minus 1 plus j.val, by have hj : equals j.is Lt unfold left Comb Vertex Count omega⟩.
correspondence.leanType: def leafVertex {r : ℕ} (j : Fin r) : Fin (leftCombVertexCount r)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A vertex is internal in the left-comb layout. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, and v of type fin left comb vertex count r, its defining expression is v.val < r minus 1.
correspondence.leanType: def IsInternal {r : ℕ} (v : Fin (leftCombVertexCount r)) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A leaf lies below a given vertex.
correspondence.leanType: def leafBelow {r : ℕ} (v : Fin (leftCombVertexCount r)) (j : Fin r) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A vertex lies on the path from leaf j to the root. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, j of type fin r, and v of type fin left comb vertex count r, its defining expression is v equals leaf Vertex j or Is Internal r : equals r v and v.val < r minus j.val.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{onPathToRoot}(j,v)=v = \operatorname{leafVertex} j \lor (\operatorname{IsInternal} (r = r) v \land v.\operatorname{val} < r - j.\operatorname{val})
correspondence.leanType: def onPathToRoot {r : ℕ} (j : Fin r) (v : Fin (leftCombVertexCount r)) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The path from leaf j to the root, including the leaf and root. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, and j of type fin r, its defining expression is Finset.univ.filter the function sending v to on Path To Root j v.
correspondence.leanType: def pathToRoot {r : ℕ} (j : Fin r) : Finset (Fin (leftCombVertexCount r))

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: x is a proper descendant of v in the left-comb tree. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, and v, x of type fin left comb vertex count r, its defining expression is Is Internal r : equals r v and x.val < r minus 1 and v.val < x.val or r minus 1 is at most x.val and x.val < 2 times r minus v.val minus 1.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{isDescendant}(v,x)=\operatorname{IsInternal} (r = r) v \land ((x.\operatorname{val} < r - 1 \land v.\operatorname{val} < x.\operatorname{val}) \lor (r - 1 \le x.\operatorname{val} \land x.\operatorname{val} < 2 \cdot r - v.\operatorname{val} - 1))
correspondence.leanType: def isDescendant {r : ℕ} (v x : Fin (leftCombVertexCount r)) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proper descendants of a vertex. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, and v of type fin left comb vertex count r, its defining expression is Finset.univ.filter the function sending x to is Descendant v x.
correspondence.leanType: def descendants {r : ℕ} (v : Fin (leftCombVertexCount r)) : Finset (Fin (leftCombVertexCount r))

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Sum of the input weights under the subtree rooted at v. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, v of type fin left comb vertex count r, and w of type fin r maps to the real numbers, its defining expression is Finset.univ.filter fun j : Fin r maps to leaf Below v j.sum the function sending j to w j.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{subtreeWeight}(v,w)=(\operatorname{Finset}.\operatorname{univ}.\operatorname{filter} \operatorname{fun} j \colon \operatorname{Fin} r => \operatorname{leafBelow} v j).\operatorname{sum} j \longmapsto w j
correspondence.leanType: def subtreeWeight {r : ℕ} (v : Fin (leftCombVertexCount r)) (w : Fin r → ℝ) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Sum of the powers on the proper descendants of v. In explicit terms, for r, s in the natural numbers, β of type comb index r s, and v of type fin left comb vertex count r, its defining expression is descendants v.sum the function sending x to β.power x.
correspondence.leanType: def descendantPowerSum {r s : ℕ} (β : CombIndex r s) (v : Fin (leftCombVertexCount r)) : ℕ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The single-vertex defect coefficient C_v(β). Mathematically this is n / 2 + W_v - 2 * sum_{x in Desc(v)} β_x - β_v - 1. In explicit terms, for r, s in the natural numbers, n in the real numbers, w of type fin r maps to the real numbers, β of type comb index r s, and v of type fin left comb vertex count r, its defining expression is n divided by 2 plus subtree Weight v w minus 2 times descendant Power Sum β v minus β.power v minus 1.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{defectCoeff}(n,w,β,v)=n / 2 + \operatorname{subtreeWeight} v w - 2 \cdot \operatorname{descendantPowerSum} β v - β.\operatorname{power} v - 1
correspondence.leanType: def defectCoeff {r s : ℕ} (n : ℝ) (w : Fin r → ℝ) (β : CombIndex r s) (v : Fin (leftCombVertexCount r)) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The contribution of one vertex to a lowering recurrence operator. In explicit terms, for r, s in the natural numbers, n in the real numbers, w of type fin r maps to the real numbers, v of type fin left comb vertex count r, and a of type comb coeff space r s plus 1, its defining expression is fun β maps to defect Coeff n w β v times A β.add At v.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{lowerAtVertex}(n,w,v,A)=\operatorname{fun} β => \operatorname{defectCoeff} n w β v \cdot A (β.\operatorname{addAt} v)
correspondence.leanType: def lowerAtVertex {r s : ℕ} (n : ℝ) (w : Fin r → ℝ) (v : Fin (leftCombVertexCount r)) (A : CombCoeffSpace r (s + 1)) : CombCoeffSpace r s

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The defect coefficient B_j(β) for the j-th input slot. In explicit terms, for r, s in the natural numbers, n in the real numbers, w of type fin r maps to the real numbers, a of type comb coeff space r s plus 1, j of type fin r, and β of type comb index r s, its defining expression is path To Root j.sum the function sending v to defect Coeff n w β v times A β.add At v.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{pathDefectCoeff}(n,w,A,j,β)=(\operatorname{pathToRoot} j).\operatorname{sum} v \longmapsto \operatorname{defectCoeff} n w β v \cdot A (β.\operatorname{addAt} v)
correspondence.leanType: def pathDefectCoeff {r s : ℕ} (n : ℝ) (w : Fin r → ℝ) (A : CombCoeffSpace r (s + 1)) (j : Fin r) (β : CombIndex r s) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The first differential d_1 A = (R_1 A,..., R_r A). In explicit terms, for r, s in the natural numbers, n in the real numbers, w of type fin r maps to the real numbers, and a of type comb coeff space r s plus 1, its defining expression is fun j β maps to path Defect Coeff n w A j β.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{dOne}(n,w,A)=\operatorname{fun} j β => \operatorname{pathDefectCoeff} n w A j β
correspondence.leanType: def dOne {r s : ℕ} (n : ℝ) (w : Fin r → ℝ) (A : CombCoeffSpace r (s + 1)) : Fin r → CombCoeffSpace r s

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Slot-wise recurrence at level s+1. In explicit terms, for r, s in the natural numbers, n in the real numbers, w of type fin r maps to the real numbers, and a of type comb coeff space r s plus 1, its defining expression is for every j : Fin r β : Comb Index r s, path Defect Coeff n w A j β equals 0.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{RankRecurrenceAtLevel}(n,w,A)=\forall (j \colon \operatorname{Fin} r) (β \colon \operatorname{CombIndex} r s), \operatorname{pathDefectCoeff} n w A j β = 0
correspondence.leanType: def RankRecurrenceAtLevel {r s : ℕ} (n : ℝ) (w : Fin r → ℝ) (A : CombCoeffSpace r (s + 1)) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Rank recurrence for a coefficient system of total degree k. At k = 0 there is no lowering recurrence.
correspondence.leanType: def RankRecurrence {r k : ℕ} (n : ℝ) (w : Fin r → ℝ) (A : CombCoeffSpace r k) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r, s in the natural numbers, n in the real numbers, w of type Fin r maps to the real numbers, and A of type Comb Coeff Space r s plus 1, Rank Recurrence At Level n w A if and only if d One n w A equals fun j maps to fun β maps to 0.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r,s\colon \mathbb{N},\;n\colon \mathbb{R},\;w\colon \operatorname{Fin} r \longrightarrow \mathbb{R},\;A\colon \operatorname{CombCoeffSpace} r (s + 1),\quad \operatorname{RankRecurrenceAtLevel} n w A \Longleftrightarrow \operatorname{dOne} n w A = \operatorname{fun} _j => (\operatorname{fun} _β => 0)
correspondence.leanType: theorem recurrence_iff_dOne_zero {r s : ℕ} (n : ℝ) (w : Fin r → ℝ) (A : CombCoeffSpace r (s + 1)) : RankRecurrenceAtLevel n w A ↔ dOne n w A = fun _j => (fun _β => 0)

references.locator: Main ambient construction and tangentiality results
references.note: Motivation for rank-general nested Laplacian words; the Lean tree complex goes beyond what is currently instantiated.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
